case_memory.prior_information_summary: 27-year-old female patient admitted via emergency room for assessment. Chief concern: onychia and paronychia of finger. Symptoms: intermittent pain, redness, swelling, pus from index and middle fingers. History: chronic nail biting and frequent hand washing. Knowledge base: paronychia is inflammation around nail, acute form often bacterial (Staphylococcus aureus), chronic form often fungal (Candida albicans). Risk factors: hand washing, trauma to cuticle. Treatment: antibiotics or antifungals, incision and drainage if pus. Patient is married, white, English-speaking, private insurance.

negative_experience.situation: When a patient presents with constipation followed by diarrhea with mucus, cramping, syncope, and ankle pain, especially in an elderly female, and initial labs are unavailable, the doctor should avoid premature diagnosis without imaging or targeted physical exam.
negative_experience.action: Request imaging of the abdomen and pelvis to evaluate for bowel obstruction or diverticulitis, and perform a focused physical exam including assessment of distension, tenderness, and pulses in the lower extremities.
negative_experience.boundary: Use this memory when the patient has constipation followed by diarrhea with mucus, cramping, syncope, and ankle pain, and when labs are unavailable or inconclusive; do not use if imaging is contraindicated or if the patient has clear signs of fracture on physical exam or prior imaging.

negative_experience.situation: When a patient reports syncope after straining with bowel symptoms and has a history of itching and mild fever, the doctor should not finalize diagnosis without considering systemic or infectious causes, especially if there is no clear gastrointestinal explanation.
negative_experience.action: Order labs including CBC, electrolytes, and inflammatory markers, and consider imaging if obstruction or infection is suspected.
negative_experience.boundary: Use this memory when syncope occurs after straining with bowel symptoms and the patient has itching and mild fever; do not use if labs are already available and show no systemic abnormalities or if imaging confirms no obstruction.
